prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-mRoAie.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: choose event-driven vs direct calls architecture, define core types/interfaces, implement base notification service, create email adapter stub

parameters.summary: Unified event-driven notification system using EventBus with pluggable NotificationAdapter interface
parameters.context: The codebase has a mixed pattern: order:created uses EventBus while order:status-changed uses CallbackRegistry. The architecture docs state event-driven is the intended pattern. We need to unify on one approach and add adapter abstraction for notification channels.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is already the primary pattern endorsed by the architecture docs. It provides event logging, async support, and decoupled communication. The adapter pattern on top allows multiple notification channels (email, webhook, SMS) without modifying the core service. All events flow through EventBus.emit(); adapters implement NotificationAdapter interface to handle delivery.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Tighter coupling, no event log/replay, synchronous only, conflicts with stated architecture preference for EventBus
The existing mixed pattern is explicitly flagged as problematic in code comments. Maintaining two patterns increases cognitive load and inconsistency.

constraints: All notification adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface
All notifications must originate from EventBus events — no direct service calls
Events must carry all data handlers need (no additional lookups)
Adapters are registered with NotificationService and dispatched by channel
assumptions: 11 more developers will build on this foundation
Multiple notification channels will be added (webhook, SMS, in-app)
The CallbackRegistry pattern for status changes can be fully replaced

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Unify notification system on event-driven architecture with adapter pattern

Choose EventBus as the single notification pattern, removing the mixed
CallbackRegistry approach. All notification triggers now flow through
EventBus.emit(), and delivery is delegated to pluggable NotificationAdapter
implementations.

Key changes:
- Add core notification types (NotificationType, NotificationChannel, etc.)
- Define NotificationAdapter interface for delivery channels
- Create EmailAdapter stub implementing NotificationAdapter
- Refactor NotificationService to be fully event-driven with adapter support
- Migrate OrderService status changes from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
- Update tests to reflect unified event-driven pattern

Constraints for other developers:
- All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface
- All notifications must originate from EventBus events
- Events must carry all data handlers need (no additional lookups)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

parameters.summary: Agent-1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven via EventBus, adapter pattern for channels, EmailAdapter stub
parameters.detail: Unified notification system on EventBus (removed CallbackRegistry usage). Created: src/models/notification.ts (core types), src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts (adapter interface), src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub email adapter), refactored src/services/notification.service.ts (fully event-driven + adapter dispatch). Migrated OrderService to emit status-changed events via EventBus. All 71 tests pass. Constraints: all adapters implement NotificationAdapter, all notifications originate from EventBus events, events carry all needed data.

parameters.summary: All notification adapters MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface from src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
parameters.detail: The NotificationAdapter interface defines: readonly channel (NotificationChannel), and send(payload) -> Promise<NotificationResult>. Adapters are registered with NotificationService.registerAdapter(). One adapter per channel.

parameters.summary: All notifications MUST originate from EventBus events — no direct service calls or CallbackRegistry
parameters.detail: The NotificationService subscribes to EventBus events in registerEventHandlers(). To add a new notification trigger: 1) Define the event type in src/events/event-types.ts, 2) Add a handler in NotificationService.registerEventHandlers(), 3) Emit the event from the originating service via EventBus.emit(). Do NOT use CallbackRegistry or direct calls.

fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1209d69
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter — interface for all notification delivery channels.
+ *
+ * CONSTRAINT: All notification adapters MUST implement this interface.
+ * The NotificationService dispatches to adapters by channel; each adapter
+ * handles the transport-specific delivery (email, webhook, SMS, etc.).
+ *
+ * Adapters are registered with NotificationService via registerAdapter().
+ * Multiple adapters can be registered for different channels.
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /**
+   * The channel this adapter handles.
+   */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this channel.
+   * Must return a result indicating success or failure.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}
